text: Now I need to check the state of the Linear issues these PRs close. Let me get all Linear issues and then match them:

text: Now I need to find which Linear issues from the PRs are not in 'done' state:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":";; Create a map of Linear issue id -> issue for quick lookup
(def linear-map (into {} (map (fn [issue] [(:id issue) issue]) linear-issues)))

;; Filter PRs to find those closing Linear issues not in 'done' state
(def filtered-prs
  (filter (fn [pr]
            (let [linear-id (:closes_linear pr)
                  issue (get linear-map linear-id)]
              (and issue (not= "d…(+79)

data: {"value":8,"defined":["linear-map","filtered-prs"],"defs":{"filtered-prs":{"count":8,"peek":{"number":1002,"repo":"acme/web","title":"Mobile crash on iOS 18 cold start: guard…","author":"dave"}}}}

text: Great! Now let me extract the required information: PR number, Linear issue id it closes, and the total count:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":";; Create the report with PR number and Linear issue id
(def report
  (map (fn [pr]
         {:pr_number (:number pr)
          :linear_issue_id (:closes_linear pr)
          :linear_issue_state (:state (get linear-map (:closes_linear pr)))})
       filtered-prs))

;; Return the count and the list
{:total_count (count report)
 :matches report}"}
